title: Thelypteris × occulta
taxonomy: Thelypteris × occulta is a nothospecies of fern, resulting from the hybridization of two different Thelypteris species. This hybrid fern is classified as a member of the Thelypteridaceae family. Thelypteris × occulta is further categorized as a member of the genus Thelypteris, which consists of a diverse group of species found in various parts of the world. The classification of Thelypteris × occulta is primarily based on its unique combination of characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. As a nothospecies, its classification is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions.

intro: Thelypteris × occulta, also known as the Hidden Lady Fern or Occult Lady Fern, is a sterile hybrid species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris × occulta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
